_note: JSON sidecar for migration-manifest.yaml. The wizard runtime CLI consumes JSON; YAML is the human-facing companion. Both files MUST stay in sync.

artifact_notes..claude/commit_hygiene.sh: An always-on guard that keeps a clean baseline before building and commits each accepted phase as its own revertable unit.
artifact_notes..gitignore: New — the emitted .gitignore now also covers the consent/runtime artifacts external-write capabilities generate automatically (acceptance receipts, run envelopes, invocation ledgers, the capability acceptance log): local operational records, not something the operator needs to decide about committing.
artifact_notes.CLAUDE.md: New — the update-notice prose now separates the informational "see what's new" read (authorizes only the check; always returns the operator to the choice, never carries forward as permission) from the separate, explicit approval that naming applying requires, so the two are never conflated into one implicit yes.
artifact_notes.agents/cron/cron_config.md: New "Standing automation runners (unattended jobs)" section: documents that every standing-automation runner must delegate its entire argv handling to standing_automation.py's run_standing_automation rather than parsing its own flags, and why (a real hand-rolled runner once silently ignored an unrecognized flag and ran a live job anyway).
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/acceptance_cer: The sole deterministic writer that flips a built capability's descriptor from not-accepted to accepted, once the operator has approved it. Now also hashes the capability module's own source file (not just its adapter/contract dependencies), so a rewrite of the capability's own logic — not only its declared dependencies — invalidates a prior acceptance and is caught by lifecycle_state's staleness check rather than passing silently.
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/adapter_regist: Extended with an OPTIONAL grant_preflight(self, token_info) hook an adapter may define: a safe, read-only, offline introspection of an already-obtained OAuth token-introspection response, answering only "does the current token already carry the op_kind's declared read-only scope" — never a write, and never an operation this method itself performs.
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/adapters.py: Extended to wire the offline scope grant-check into the adapter dispatch path (check_scope_grant / GRANT_STATUS_NOT_GRANTED), so a declared OAuth scope can be checked against a token-introspection response before any live use is attempted.
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/adapters_gmail: All four Gmail op_kinds now declare and check the same read-only scope (gmail.readonly) through the new grant_preflight hook, so a missing or too-narrow Gmail scope is caught offline instead of surfacing as a live-run failure.
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/bounds.py: New — the overall ceiling on how much a single run may change at once, scaled to how reversible the action is.
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/capability_api: New — the only import surface your capability code should use to write back or read from a connected account, so capability code never has a path to the credential-holding adapter itself.
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/capability_hea: The composite, AST-first, read-only capability health-check primitive: for each capability, checks whether its source is scanner-clean, whether its module still imports cleanly, whether it is paused or has a migration request queued, and whether its acceptance is stale (an accepted capability whose code changed since approval — a conformant rebuild, or an older record with no verifiable hash), so a capability that fails any one of those checks is reported unhealthy — not merely "paused" — before the operator is ever invited into it. Still strictly read-only: it surfaces the same verdict lifecycle_state/upgrade_reconcile already compute, never forces the change itself.
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/capability_ide: New — the fail-closed CapabilityIdentity resolver: builds a canonical index of every emitted capability by construction (never guessed) and enforces a four-way identity coherence check — descriptor id / capability id / mechanism id / module stem must all agree; a capability's own external-system surface is deliberately excluded from the check — before registration, acceptance, health, or migration-close ever trust an id. An id that cannot be resolved yet (a not-yet-built capability) is not refused; an id that resolves to a DIFFERENT capability is.
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/capability_inv: New — the D-Layer-1 deterministic capability-invariant battery, run at next-phase Step 4 before any live trial: a fixed set of build-time checks, including two test-quality probes (a producer-entrypoint check confirming the capability's own code path is actually exercised by its tests, and a known-bad-fails check confirming the test suite would actually catch a real regression) that must all pass, fail-closed, before a newly built capability is trusted enough to trial live.
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/capability_reg: Registers an operator-originated capability (one the operator asks for mid-run) into the descriptor set with its risk class and protections. A descriptor id that resolves — via an already-emitted capability module's own stem or its declared surface — to a DIFFERENT capability than the one being registered is now refused, via a real four-way identity coherence check (capability_identity.assert_identity_coherent); a not-yet-built capability id is not refused.
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/consent_narrat: New — builds the consent sentence you approve from the actual facts of what the run will do, and keeps the plain-language narration matched to what the mechanism actually enforces.
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/contracts.py: Extended with the Gmail operation kinds and their risk classes.
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/copy_run_proof: The pre-acceptance copy-run proof now checks the capability's evidence against the real external surface (proof time), so a capability cannot be proven by code that merely ran without confirming the effect landed.
Build-time gate: checks that every declared write-back capability has a matching risk descriptor before a phase can be built (declaration coverage, not acceptance).
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/effects_manife: Binds a capability's approved risk profile to the exact adapter code that will run it, so approving one adapter can never silently cover a different one.
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/evidence.py: New — defines the per-operation-kind proof an adapter must return: what counts as evidence, checked against the real account, that a write actually landed and that an undo actually restored.
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/lifecycle_stat: New — the capability lifecycle reconciler: keeps a capability's paused/accepted state internally consistent through idempotent reconciliation (a rebuild or migration forces accepted:false until the operator re-trials it; a conformant rebuild that changed the capability's own code is detected as stale and its acceptance is revoked, not silently carried forward), runs the sanctioned complete_migration step in fail-safe order, and appends to the acceptance audit log on every re-acceptance so the history is never silently overwritten.
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/operations.py: Generalized run path now threads the real-account verification result through to the run envelope, so a bulk run records not just what was written but whether each write was confirmed against the account.
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/operator_accep: Imports registered_adapters.py at the top of the module (Task 7 / F-37), which fires every adapter module's registration before the CLI's own logic runs — the fix that makes the documented operator-acceptance command turnkey for a freshly-declared capability, not just for already-import-triggered ones. Also now surfaces the outcome of closing a pending migration request during acceptance (what closed, and any identity-resolution problem hit along the way) instead of a silently-swallowed bit, so an unresolved or ambiguous migration id is never a silent no-op.
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/read_facade.py: Gives a capability a read-only view of a connected account, so the code that builds and tests a capability can never reach the credential that can write.
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/read_facades_g: New — the read-only Gmail view your capability code sees, kept in its own file with no adapter or credential in it, so nothing in it can ever be repurposed to write.
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/registered_ada: New — the build-emitted static adapter registry that the operator-acceptance CLI imports at module scope, so importing one module fires every shipped AND capability-added adapter module's registration once, up front. Fixes a real gap: without this, the prescribed turnkey acceptance command refused every freshly-declared capability with "no registered contract for op_kind ..." because nothing had imported its adapter module yet in that fresh process.
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/run_envelope.p: mint_run_envelope now accepts a versioned reviewed_set: the v1 shape (any non-empty list of mappings) is unchanged and still the default, and a new v2 shape — the one triage.py's judgment-path tool produces — additionally requires a unique unit_id, a reason_shown, and stronger per-entry guarantees the operator's review actually depended on, so a triage-produced reviewed_set is bound to what the operator was actually shown.
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/scan.py: Extended to check credential handling as well as write-path bypasses, and to apply the new trust-zone split so only adapter code (never capability code) is allowed to construct a write credential.
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/standing_autom: The safe standing-automation entrypoint. A recurring or unattended job (a filter sweep, a recurring digest email, ...) must delegate its ENTIRE command-line decision to run_standing_automation here rather than parsing its own flags: an unrecognized flag now exits non-zero and touches nothing external, and a first-class --check/--dry-run preview lets a schedule or the operator confirm a job is safe to run without ever running it live. Also now refuses — with its own distinct exit code — when the job's capability is currently accepted but its code changed since approval (a conformant rebuild), before build_operation, run_live, or the client are ever touched, so a scheduled job never runs stale, unreviewed code.
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/triage.py: New — the read-only judgment-path triage tool: groups write-back candidates, classifies each into a small generic vocabulary (safe / suspect / protected), and itemizes exceptions, producing exactly the reviewed_set-v2 shape run_envelope.py's mint_run_envelope expects. Performs no external write of any kind — no adapter import, no run_operation reference, no credential — it only classifies data the caller already collected.
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/write_gate.py: Runtime gate now backs the run envelope with one persistent, atomic invocation ledger and reserves against the cap before the first write, so a run's total blast radius is bounded even across many actions. Extended so a paused capability's op_kind is a fail-closed deny on every sanctioned route — not just the ones checked incidentally — so a capability an update has paused can never be run around. Still recognizes the capped live-sample and no-mutation dry-run test targets by live blast radius, not by the word "test".
artifact_notes.agents/lib/external_write/zones.py: Extended with trust-zone exemptions for the build-emitted primitives (registered_adapters.py, standing_automation.py), documenting exactly why each is allowed to do what it does (module-scope adapter import; a legitimate dry_run-target run_operation call) without being mistaken for a capability-zone violation. Internal decision-record ID references in the module's own comments were scrubbed to plain language (no internal ID leaks into emitted content, per the public-boundary scrub).
artifact_notes.docs/document_impact_map.md: Records the operator-originated-capability registration as a document-impacting event.
artifact_notes.operating_discipline.md: Updated so off-plan requests are routed into the enhancement flow, and update notices are carved out of the single-next-step rule. Also updated so a saved next step — including a paused or half-finished task — always leads at the start of a session or after a pause, and so saved state records only the version currently deployed, never a forward-looking prediction about a future update check. Session-start orientation consults the capability health-check before surfacing or resuming a capability, and refuses to invite the operator into one the check reports import-broken. New: the assistant also relays any safety notice the system itself produces — for example when a capability's ability to write outside the project has been switched off — faithfully and verbatim, never summarizing, softening, or dropping a caveat, especially a "do not rely on this" boundary caveat.
artifact_notes.project_instructions.md: Updated to describe the enhancement flow and its voice rules for handling off-plan requests.
artifact_notes.quality/capability_descriptor_registry.m: A readable view of the capability descriptor set for quality review. Operator-reviewed on upgrade.
artifact_notes.quality/co-protected-workflows.md: Now lists the workflows tied to each registered capability so co-protected steps are visible. Operator-reviewed on upgrade.
artifact_notes.requirements.txt: New — pins the Python dependencies your writes-back system actually needs. Only present for a system that writes back to something outside itself; a read-only system has no Python dependency and gets no requirements.txt.
artifact_notes.security/capability_descriptors.json: New machine-readable record of every write-back capability your system has, its risk class, and whether you have accepted it. Operator-reviewed on upgrade (never overwritten).
artifact_notes.security/credentials_registry.md: Adds three OAuth-only columns — Declared scope, Needs admin grant, and Scope status — that separate whether a credential's VALUE is stored (Active) from whether its specific declared scope has actually been checked and proven to work (deny-by-default: verified only after a real, recorded use of exactly that scope has succeeded). Fixes a real incident where a credential was recorded as working from a check against a broader scope than the one actually used.
artifact_notes.security/gitignore_manifest.md: New — the gitignore manifest table documents the same new operational (local-only) consent/runtime artifacts as no-decision-needed entries, so the operator can see why each is excluded without having to inspect .gitignore itself.
artifact_notes.session_bootstrap.md: Now leads with the saved next step — including a paused or half-finished task — instead of an unrelated item picked from a date list.
artifact_notes.wizard/skills/add-capability.md: Now also checks for a mechanism an update safely paused because it no longer followed a current safety rule, and offers to bring it back through this same define-build-accept flow.
artifact_notes.wizard/skills/credential-setup.md: Adds a new Step 0 (org-admin grant first, for an OAuth credential whose Needs admin grant is Yes — handled before anything else for that credential, never discovered mid-trial) and rewrites Step 4 (Verify) to run the offline scope grant-check first for any credential with a declared scope, before ever calling anything live.
artifact_notes.wizard/skills/next-phase.md: Adds a live-trial-readiness gate in Step 3: for each credential a phase's agents actually use with a declared OAuth scope, checks Scope status before the phase's supervised live trial — a not-yet-granted scope withholds only the trial and downstream live authorization (never the build itself, never Steps 1–4), and files a stub_tracker entry with the exact re-check command. Also adds a Step 4 deterministic self-check: before a phase's supervised live trial, runs the D-Layer-1 capability-invariant battery (capability_invariants.py) and fails closed — refusing the trial — on any invariant failure, rather than proceeding on an unverified build.
artifact_notes.wizard/skills/triage-review.md: New — the operator-facing skill that runs the read-only judgment-path triage tool (triage.py) over a set of write-back candidates, shows the operator the grouped/classified result and any itemized exceptions, and on approval produces the reviewed_set-v2 that mint_run_envelope binds the run to — so what the operator approves is exactly and only what the run does.
artifact_notes.work/stub_tracker.md: The Credential stub type now also covers a declared OAuth scope that is not yet granted (not just a key not yet obtained or an OAuth flow not yet configured), pointing at the credentials registry's Scope status column and the Credential Setup skill.

migrations.migration_steps: 1. Run the update check (it reports what's new + the recommendation). 2. On the operator's yes, the assistant applies it ("${{WIZARD_HOME:-$HOME/agent-wizard}}/scripts/wizard" self-upgrade --to v0.14.0 --apply). 3. A backup is taken first; the operator's own data, rules, credentials, and logs are never touched.
migrations.notes: Minor-additive release to v0.14.0 (Phase 3 Cut 1 self-QA slice): adds a fail-closed capability-identity resolver (capability_identity.py) enforcing a four-way descriptor/capability/mechanism/module-stem coherence check — surface deliberately excluded — at registration, acceptance, and health time, so a descriptor id that resolves to a different capability than the one being registered is refused rather than silently trusted; an idempotent lifecycle reconciler (lifecycle_state.py) that keeps paused/accepted state coherent (a rebuild or migration forces accepted:false until the operator re-trials it, and a conformant rebuild that changed the capability's own code is detected as stale and its acceptance revoked rather than carried forward silently) and appends to the acceptance audit log on every re-acceptance; and the D-Layer-1 deterministic capability-invariant battery plus test-quality probes (capability_invariants.py), run at next-phase Step 4 and fail-closed before any live trial. Also scrubs internal decision-record IDs out of emitted content and adds .gitignore coverage for the consent/runtime artifacts these mechanisms generate. No breaking changes; all existing capabilities are byte-for-byte unaffected and all prior operator project files remain valid.
migrations.preflight: Verify the operator project carries a .wizard/manifest.json whose foundation_bundle_version is v0.13.1 (or any version in the supported range). A backup is taken automatically.
migrations.rollback: Restore from .wizard/backups/pre-v0.14.0/ and set foundation_bundle_version back to v0.13.1.

No breaking changes; all existing capabilities are byte-for-byte unaffected and all prior operator project files remain valid.

an idempotent lifecycle reconciler (lifecycle_state.py) that keeps paused/accepted state coherent (a rebuild or migration forces accepted:false until the operator re-trials it, and a conformant rebuild that changed the capability's own code is detected as stale and its acceptance revoked rather than carried forward silently) and appends to the acceptance audit log on every re-acceptance; and the D-Layer-1 deterministic capability-invariant battery plus test-quality probes (capability_invariants.py), run at next-phase Step 4 and fail-closed before any live trial. Also scrubs internal decision-record IDs out of emitted content and adds .gitignore coverage for the consent/runtime artifacts these mechanisms generate). No breaking changes; all existing capabilities are byte-for-byte unaffected and all prior operator project files remain valid.
migrations.preflight: Verify the operator project carries a .wizard/manifest.json whose foundation_bundle_version is v0.10.1 (or any version in the supported range). A backup is taken automatically.
migrations.rollback: Restore from .wizard/backups/pre-v0.14.0/ and set foundation_bundle_version back to v0.10.1.
